hypothesis: Replacing the canonical SMILES anchor with stereochemistry-preserving SELFIES representations that explicitly encode chiral centers and E/Z double-bond configurations will induce enantiomer-discriminating pose invariance in the 3D encoder, such that the contrastive model achieves greater than 90% Accuracy@10 for retrieving correct stereoisomers under unseen SO(3) rotations while simultaneously reducing retrieval confusion between enantiomeric pairs—a failure mode that the current SMILES-anchored framework cannot resolve because canonical SMILES representations are insensitive to spatial chirality.
